perturbation.original_text: 1.2. Development Fees. As payment for Developer's satisfactory performance of the services set forth in Schedule 1 hereto (the "Technology Development Services"), DSS shall pay Developer the sum of US $23,000 per month, for the duration of the Term hereof, with payments to commence on March 1, 2018.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. Development Fees. As payment for Developer's satisfactory performance of the services set forth in Schedule 1 hereto (the "Technology Development Services"), DSS shall pay Developer the sum of US $23,000 per month, for the first six months of the Term hereof, with payments to commence on March 1, 2018. Subsequent payments will be determined based on a mutually agreed-upon revised schedule.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the fixed monthly payments to the first six months, while the rest of the contract refers to a consistent monthly payment for the entire term. This contradicts Section 2.1, which discusses the 'Term' of the agreement being 12 months initially, which will automatically renew for one-month periods thereafter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. Term. The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date, and shall continue thereafter for a period of twelve (12) months (the "Initial Term"). The Initial Term shall automatically renew for one-month periods thereafter unless either party provides 30- days advance notice of termination, unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 2.2 hereof. For purposes hereof, the Initial Term, together with any extension or renewal terms, shall hereinafter be collectively referred to as the "Term".
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text specifies a fixed monthly payment for the 'duration of the Term.' The changed text limits this fixed payment to the first six months, with subsequent payments based on a revised schedule. This directly contradicts Section 2.1, which defines the 'Term' as an initial 12-month period with automatic one-month renewals. The change creates a direct conflict between the payment schedule and the duration of the agreement, making it unclear how payments are to be calculated after the initial six months, therefore there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1. Term. The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date, and shall continue thereafter for a period of twelve (12) months (the "Initial Term"). The Initial Term shall automatically renew for one-month periods thereafter unless either party provides 30- days advance notice of termination, unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 2.2 hereof. For purposes hereof, the Initial Term, together with any extension or renewal terms, shall hereinafter be collectively referred to as the "Term".
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1. Term. The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date, and shall continue thereafter for a period of twelve (12) months (the "Initial Term"). The Initial Term shall automatically renew for one-month periods thereafter unless either party provides 30- days advance notice of termination, unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 2.2 hereof. For purposes hereof, the Initial Term, together with any extension or renewal terms, shall hereinafter be collectively referred to as the "Term". However, Developer has the right to terminate this agreement at any time, without cause, with no penalty.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause stating the Developer can terminate the agreement at any time without penalty. This directly contradicts section 2.2 'Early Termination' which outlines specific conditions and notice periods required for termination, implying penalties if those are not met. This creates an in-text contradiction about the conditions under which the contract can be terminated and if any penalty fees are involved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2. Early Termination. 2.2.1. Either party may terminate this Agreement prior to expiration of the Term: (i) upon thirty (30) days prior written notice, or (ii) immediately upon written notice to the other party if: (a) the other party declares or a petition is filed in any court for insolvency or bankruptcy and such petition is not dismissed in thirty (30) days; (b) the other party reorganizes under the relevant bankruptcy act or any similar statute in such party's jurisdiction of incorporation; (c) the other party consents to the appointment of a trustee in bankruptcy or a receiver or similar entity; or (d) the Developer breaches DSS's Technology or Intellectual Property rights contained herein. 2.2.2. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, (i) the Technology Development Services License granted to Developer hereunder shall immediately cease, and (ii) Developer shall immediately cease use of all proprietary technology files heretofore delivered by DSS and shall deliver to DSS all such proprietary files along with any and all Improvements completed to date by Developer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives the 'Developer' the right to terminate the agreement at any time, without cause, with no penalty. This directly contradicts section 2.2 'Early Termination' which outlines specific conditions and notice periods required for termination. The changed text also creates contradiction about penalties for early termination. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction.
